sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The left dot moved from (32-33, 18-19) to (32-33, 28-29). That's 10 columns right in 5 clicks (2 columns per click). The dot is now at (32-33, 28-29), which is just to the left of the blue shape at (23-31, 29-37). The blue shape's left edge is at column 29, and the dot's right edge is at column 29. So they're touching!
But the level didn't complete. Let me check if the dot needs to be at a specific position or if all 4 dots need to touch the blue shape.
Looking at the image, the left dot is now adjacent to the blue shape. Let me move the other 3 dots to touch the blue shape as well.
Current positions:
- Left: (32-33, 28-29) - touching the blue shape's left edge
- Right: (36-37, 40-41) - needs to move left and up
- Bottom right: (53-54, 48-49) - needs to move left and up
- Bottom left: (54-55, 13-14) - needs to move right and up
The blue shape is at (23-31, 29-37). Let me move the right dot to touch the blue shape's right edge.
The right dot is at (36-37, 40-41). The blue shape's right edge is at column 37. The dot needs to move left by ~3 columns and up by ~5 rows.
Let me move the right dot left and up.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Left dot now at (32-33, 28-29), touching blue shape's left edge at col 29
# Need to move other 3 dots to touch the blue shape
# Blue shape: rows 23-31, cols 29-37

# Right dot at (36-37, 40-41): needs to move left to col 37 (blue right edge) and up to row 31
# Move left first: click at decreasing column positions to the left of the dot
# Then move up: click at decreasing row positions above the dot

# Let me move the right dot left by 2 columns (from col 40 to col 38)
# Then move it up to touch the blue shape

# Right dot: click left to move it left
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 37, 'col': 39},  # move left
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 35, 'col': 39},  # move up
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 39},  # move up
])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
magenta = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4]
print("After moving right dot:")
for n in magenta:
    if n['boundary'][0][0] > 3:
        print(f"  Magenta: boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("Game over:", last_action_result.get('game_over'))
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
